program_name: Engineering in Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence
campus: San Pietro in Vincoli campus, Via Eudossiana 18, Rome
faculty: Information Engineering, Informatics and Statistics (Ingegneria dell'informazione, informatica e statistica)
admission_test_type: Verification of requirements and personal preparation; CV/background assessment with a Skype/online interview for selected (non-EU MoveIN) applicants
applicant_route: non-EU residing in Italy (with residence permit)
application_platform: MoveIN pre-selection platform (sapienza.gomovein.com) for international/non-EU applicants; Infostud (Sapienza student portal) for application and enrolment; Universitaly for visa pre-enrolment (non-EU)
access_quota_non_eu: Per-programme non-EU contingent is set in the official Call for Applications; not extracted for this programme [uncertain].
application_round: September 2026 intake. Non-EU (visa) MoveIN pre-selection 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026; EU/equivalent applications open until 31 July 2026.
intake_semester_availability: September 2026 intake [uncertain] (catalogue/admissions pages describe a single September intake; February intake not confirmed for this programme).
places_and_foreign_contingent: Total places and non-EU contingent are published in the programme Call for Applications; not extracted in this pass [uncertain].

academic_requirements.summary: An undergraduate degree (e.g. Bachelor's) in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or another scientific area; applicants from other scientific areas are analysed and approved on a case-by-case basis. A strong academic background in Computer Science is expected. Selected (MoveIN) applicants are invited to an online/Skype interview to assess skills and background, including technical questions on the relevant background.
academic_requirements.source: MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027 PDF; programme catalogue page 33515

country_specific_eligibility: Foreign qualifications must be valid for enrolment per Sapienza's 'Accepted qualifications by country' (2026/2027) document. Minimum GPA 75/100 applies to MoveIN (non-EU) applicants. See https://www.uniroma1.it/sites/default/files/field_file_allegati/accepted_qualifications_by_country_26mag26.pdf
curricular_integration_policy: Applicants from scientific areas other than Computer Engineering/Computer Science are evaluated case-by-case; specific curricular integration rules for this programme were not detailed on the official sources checked [uncertain].
conditional_enrolment_policy: Applicants who have not yet graduated may apply for the requirements assessment; final admission depends on completing the Bachelor's degree before enrolment. Specific dates for this programme not separately published [uncertain].

accepted_certificates_and_scores: Cambridge English B2 First (FCE) or higher (also CAE, CPE)
Trinity College London ISE II or higher
PTE General Level 3 (B2) or higher / PTE Academic
language_requirements.waivers: Waived for native English speakers; holders of an English-taught diploma/degree from an accredited institution in EU/EEA/Schengen, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the UK or the US; holders of International Baccalaureate (IB), GCE/GCSE or comparable. Fully waived for applicants from UK, US, Canada, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand (English as primary medium of instruction).
language_requirements.source: MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027 and English language requirements 2026 PDF

language_submission_timing: Language certification (or self-certification of evidence of English training) is submitted at the MoveIN pre-selection / application stage.
italian_language_requirement: Not required for admission; the programme is taught entirely in English. Italian useful for daily life [uncertain].
application_deadline_eu: EU and EU-equivalent candidates (no visa required) may apply until 31 July 2026 for 2026/2027.
application_deadline_non_eu: Non-EU candidates residing abroad and requiring a visa: MoveIN pre-selection 22 December 2025 - 15 May 2026. Pre-accepted students must register on Universitaly by 30 June 2026.
application_fee: EUR 30 per submitted application (maximum 2 applications per candidate per academic year); no fee waiver available.
visa_preenrolment_deadline: Pre-accepted non-EU students must register/pre-enrol on the Universitaly platform by 30 June 2026.
required_documents: English language certificate (or self-certification of English training)
Additional supporting documents as requested in the programme call
CV (used by the committee to assess background)
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents must be valid per Sapienza's 'Accepted qualifications by country' rules; legalization/Declaration of Value/CIMEA statement may be required for foreign qualifications. Specific authentication detail not separately listed for this programme [uncertain].

entry_exam_or_test.type: No standardized written entrance exam. Admission is by verification of requirements and personal preparation; selected MoveIN (non-EU) applicants undergo a CV/background assessment plus an online/Skype interview.
entry_exam_or_test.source: Catalogue page 33515 (Access procedure: Verification of requirements and personal preparation) and MoveIN academic requirements 2026-2027

test_variant_and_session: Online/Skype interview scheduled individually for selected applicants; no fixed public test session [uncertain].
test_score_conversion_or_threshold: MoveIN minimum GPA 75/100; English minimum IELTS 5.5 / TOEFL iBT 80. No numeric entrance-test threshold (no written test).

source_quotes.quote: Programme code 33515 ... Course type Master's degree ... Bachelor class LM-32 ... Access procedure Verification of requirements and personal preparation ... Faculty Ingegneria dell'informazione, informatica e statistica ... Department INGEGNERIA INFORMATICA, AUTOMATICA E GESTIONALE "ANTONIO RUBERTI" ... Language English

As a minimum requirement an applicant must have an undergraduate degree (e.g. Bachelor's) in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, or other scientific areas (the latter will be analysed and approved on a case-by-case basis). Selected students will be invited for a Skype interview ... MINIMUM GPA: 75/100 ... IELTS 5.5 or higher ... TOEFL iBT 80 or higher

the deadline for Non-EU candidates (visa applicants) expired on May 15, 2026. The application process will remain open until July 31, 2026, exclusively for EU and EU-equivalent candidates ... a EUR 30 application fee for each submitted application (up to a maximum of two applications per candidate per intake)

uncertainty_notes: Per-programme non-EU access quota and total places not extracted; must be confirmed in the programme Call for Applications.
official_call_url points to the catalogue 'Apply' section; a programme-specific bando PDF was not located in this pass.
Intake semester availability assumed September only; February intake not confirmed for this programme.
Curricular integration and conditional enrolment specifics for this programme not separately documented on checked sources.
Document authentication (Declaration of Value / CIMEA) requirement inferred from general Sapienza policy, not programme-specific text.
Italian language requirement assumed not required (English-taught), not explicitly stated on the programme page.
